findings.summary: Decoupling analysis misses C7 and C8 (22uF bulk caps) on +5V rail because their value is 'NA(22uF/6.3V/0603)'; Correctly captures Rev A schematic error: U2 (SN74ACT244) VCC pin on floating unnamed ...

correct.description: In Rev A, U2's VCC pin (pin 20) is on __unnamed_35 — a floating net not connected to +3.3V or +5V. Similarly U2 pin 13 (A2) and pin 19 (~OE) are on separate floating unnamed nets. Rev A1 corrects this: U2 VCC moves to +5V and GND to GND. The analyzer faithfully captures this difference: Rev A has 73 total nets while Rev A1 has 70 (the 3 floating unnamed nets are eliminated). The design_observations does not generate an explicit warning about the floating VCC pin, but the net data faithfully represents the schematic state.

missed.description: On the +5V rail, C7 and C8 have value 'NA(22uF/6.3V/0603)' — the 'NA' prefix followed by the actual spec in parentheses. The capacitance parser cannot extract '22uF' from this non-standard format, so these caps are excluded from the decoupling_analysis. Only C13 (value '100nF', directly parseable) is found, giving a total of 0.1uF with has_bulk=false. The design_observations then incorrectly warns that there is no bulk capacitance on +5V. The same issue affects Rev A1. The parser should attempt to extract capacitance values from parenthetical content in 'NA(...)' formatted values.

findings.summary: IOUT1 (BNC output connector) and IOUT2 (2-pin output connector) classified as 'other' instead of 'connector'

incorrect.description: IOUT1 has value 'BNC-JD11-50-LF' and lib_id 'BNC-JD11-50-LF' — it is a BNC coaxial output connector for the DAC current output signal. IOUT2 has value 'NA(HN1x2)' and lib_id 'CON2' — it is a 2-pin header. Both are classified as type 'other' because their lib_ids do not match the connector pattern recognizer. BNC-JD11-50-LF should be recognizable as a connector type. The SIG_IN1 BNC in iCE40-ADC has the same misclassification as 'switch'. These are output signal connectors for an FPGA-driven DAC board.
